behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at a module handoff boundary (request builder -> API client). A unit test of prompt-building alone can miss wiring bugs where labels are dropped before send. Rejected System because behavior text is about request content, not real network/OS effects; an in-process fake client can catch it. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because correctness does not depend on model judgment or multi-step agent decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the classifier with a fake API transport that records outbound payloads; call classify with a known taxonomy and assert the sent request includes every taxonomy label exactly once in the expected field/section.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is local validation/normalization logic on returned label. No real infra or model-quality dependency is required to reproduce. Rejected Integration because behavior text focuses on mapping invalid value -> "unknown", which can be exercised directly in local logic.
behaviors.test_description: Call the label-normalization/validation path with taxonomy {legal, technical, financial, other} and raw label "medical"; assert final output is "unknown".

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic-function behavior (same input -> same output). No cross-module invariant, infra, or model output quality is needed. Rejected higher levels because behavior explicitly scopes to identical raw responses and normalization only.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke normalization repeatedly with the same raw response fixture (including casing/whitespace variants already fixed in fixture) and assert stable identical final label every run.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires repeated live API calls and consistency of model-returned label, which depends on real model behavior/non-determinism. A stub would hide real drift/instability. Rejected Workflow because this is repeated single-step classification calls, not a chained multi-agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run N real classifications for one document/taxonomy against the live provider, storing raw provider responses and final labels; assert label consistency across calls when raw provider output is unchanged, and only allow label change when provider response content changes.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Failure appears when config/token-budget logic and dispatch wiring interact (enforce-before-send invariant). Pure unit tests can miss cases where enforcement is computed but not applied to sent request. Rejected System because no real OS/network behavior is required; fake client capture is sufficient. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because this is pre-dispatch infrastructure logic, not model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Configure a small budget, classify an over-budget document with a fake transport, and assert policy behavior (truncate or reject) is applied before any outbound send; if truncate policy, assert sent payload token estimate is <= budget.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: Requires real provider call and real provider token accounting; mocks can trivially return in-budget usage and hide true usage drift. Per rule, execution through a real AI/ML API call defaults to Agentic unless purely wrapper infra is tested; here assertion is about provider-reported usage outcome itself. Rejected Workflow because it is a single call check, not multi-step agent sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Run live classification on representative document(s), read provider-reported token usage metadata per call, and assert usage <= configured budget threshold for each call.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This is orchestration/retry behavior across module and API client boundary. In-process fake API responses (429 then success) can reproduce failure. Rejected System because real network timing is not required to validate retry decision path. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because correctness does not depend on model content or multiple agentic decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fake API client scripted to return rate-limit error first and success second; verify classifier waits/backs off per policy, retries, and returns success result instead of propagating the first error.
